DESCRIPTION: The Brazilian Law on Bidding and Administrative Contracts establishes general rules for bidding processes aimed at ensuring efficiency and transparency in the management of public resources.
FUNDAMENTAL PRINCIPLES: Legality, impartiality, morality, publicity, efficiency, equality, adherence to the bidding document, and objective judgment.
TYPES OF BIDDING: Open bidding, price quotation, invitation to bid, auction, and competitive dialogue.
PHASES OF THE BIDDING PROCESS: Planning, publication of the bidding document, receipt of proposals, judgment, qualification, award, and approval.
JUDGMENT CRITERIA: Lowest price, best technique, technique and price, highest bid or offer.
SANCTIONS AND PENALTIES: Warning, fines, temporary suspension, prohibition from contracting with the public sector, and sanctions Penalties in serious cases.
